name: Hezbollah Cross-Border Raid — 2 Soldiers Kidnapped
detail: July 12, 2006: Hezbollah units cross the Blue Line and ambush an IDF patrol, killing 8 soldiers and kidnapping Ehud Goldwasser and Eldad Regev. The operation ('True Promise') triggers the 34-day Second Lebanon War. Bodies returned in 2008 prisoner exchange.

name: Hezbollah Rockets Strike Haifa — First Time Since 1948
detail: July 13, 2006: Hezbollah fires Katyusha rockets reaching Haifa, Israel's third-largest city, killing 8 at a railway depot. Haifa had not been struck by enemy fire since 1948. Over the 34-day war, Hezbollah fires 4,228 rockets into northern Israel, killing 44 civilians and 12 soldiers.

name: C-802 Anti-Ship Missile Hits INS Hanit
detail: July 14, 2006: Hezbollah fires an Iranian-supplied C-802 anti-ship missile, striking the INS Hanit corvette operating off the Lebanese coast. 4 Israeli sailors killed; ship damaged but not sunk. The strike demonstrated Hezbollah's possession of advanced Iranian precision weaponry, shocking the Israeli military.

name: Hezbollah Opens 'Support Front' — Day After Oct 7
detail: October 8, 2023: Hezbollah begins cross-border fire exchanges in declared solidarity with Hamas after the October 7 attacks. The 11-month 'support front' involves daily anti-tank fire, rockets, and UAV attacks against northern Israel. 60,000+ Israeli civilians evacuated from border communities; 90,000+ Lebanese displaced.

name: Hezbollah Rocket Salvos After Pager/Nasrallah Attacks
detail: September–November 2024: Despite losing Nasrallah (Sep 27) and its communications infrastructure to the pager attacks (Sep 17–18), Hezbollah continued firing rockets into northern Israel and eventually reached the Tel Aviv area. Demonstrated pre-stored launch capacity operating without central command.

name: Hezbollah Drone Strike Evades Iron Dome — IDF Base Hit
detail: October 2024: Hezbollah launched multiple drones at IDF bases in northern Israel during the conflict escalation. Several successfully penetrated Iron Dome defences, wounding soldiers and demonstrating Hezbollah retained precision drone capability despite leadership losses.

name: Hezbollah Military Seizure of West Beirut
detail: May 7–9, 2008: After the Lebanese government moved to shut down Hezbollah's private telecom network and remove the Beirut airport security chief, Hezbollah deployed fighters to seize West Beirut, briefly taking over Hariri's Future Movement media outlets and threatening other opponents. 81 killed in clashes before Doha Agreement ended the crisis.

name: Islamic Jihad (Hezbollah Predecessor) — Marine Barracks Bombing
detail: October 23, 1983: Islamic Jihad Organisation (Hezbollah precursor) claims twin suicide truck bombings destroying the US Marine barracks (241 dead) and French paratroopers HQ (58 dead) at Beirut Airport. The bombings directly led to the withdrawal of the US-French-Italian Multinational Force from Lebanon.
